title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: I'm going to practice English, but I'm going to ask you a question and I'm going to answer it.
We're going to use a service called TLGB and record a live.
We're going to do it.
So if you're going to organize it, you can upload a video of what you want to do with your members.
Secondly, we're going to give you a promise that you can do better.

title: will / shall
examples: But I will always say a show is still a show.
So these are worksheets that I created that will help you practice what you learn in the video.
If you sign up for our membership, whenever we do a live, you will have access to the full video.
When we do, our members will get exclusive benefits and discounts to those services.
So I think you're curious about how it will be.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: Those are bigger numbers than I ever could have imagined.
I do think that there are better ways to learn natural English that you can use in your everyday life.
Learning some kind of English that they could use at their job or in their daily life.
But with our vlogs, I'm trying to create content that can apply to large groups of people, no matter who you are, having a morning routine, cooking a meal, exercising.
These are simple everyday things that everybody can probably relate to.

title: could have (done)
examples: Those are bigger numbers than I ever could have imagined.

title: may / might (possibility)
examples: It might not apply to your everyday life.

title: have to / must
examples: So I think that's one advantage we have to our content is, you know, we're always filming something new, using new language and slang.

title: had better / it's time
examples: Now, it's time to practice your speaking.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: I really thought almost everyone would be in Korea, but we got some comments coming from California, and different states in the US, Canada, Australia.
No idea when we made this, that it would go over so well.
I go to the gym part and if I apply it more clearly, I think it would be better.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: So the goal of this step is just to understand what's happening in the video and increase your comprehension.
Then, re-watch the video with English subtitles to see how the vocabulary is used in context.

title: Passive (special: get + p.p.)
examples: Well, I normally get up, have a cup of coffee, watch the news, and then showered first, get dressed, and go to work.
